attacks.claim: ReZero scalar at 0 → no residual; A reduces to identity (baseline)
attacks.evidence: Standard ReZero argument: alpha=0 → residual zero → effectively no module
attacks.rebuttal: Bregman duality DERIVES alpha from divergence between residual stream and baseline; alpha = grad_phi(R) - grad_phi(B) where phi is convex potential; alpha=0 iff distributions IDENTICAL, but training penalty maintains non-trivial distributional difference. The Bregman-derived alpha is NOT a learnable scalar but a structural functional of activations. Cannot collapse to 0 without distribution-collapse which training explicitly prevents.
